name: Avenue Baptist Brotherhood
description_short: Avenue Baptist Brotherhood Federal Credit Union is a small, faith-based credit union in Shreveport, Louisiana serving religious community members since 1957.
description_long:
Avenue Baptist Brotherhood Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in Shreveport, Louisiana. As a federal credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution serving members of religious organizations, specifically aligning services with spiritual values and community needs. The credit union maintains one physical branch location at 901 W 70th Street with a current membership base of over 95 loyal members.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, share draft accounts, money market accounts, and share certificates (CDs). Members also have access to financial education resources, financial workshops, and online banking tools including bill pay functionality. The credit union provides both fixed-rate mortgages (15 years or less) with mortgage calculators, and maintains a mobile banking app available on iOS and Android platforms. Competitive interest rates are prominently advertised, with current offerings including 0% APR on new vehicle loans, used vehicle loans, and unsecured credit cards, plus 11.50% on other unsecured loans.

As a small, mission-driven credit union, Avenue Baptist Brotherhood distinguishes itself through its faith-based membership eligibility requirements and community-focused approach. The institution operates with low overhead typical of smaller credit unions and emphasizes member service with dedicated staff. Their not-for-profit structure allows them to offer rates and fees competitive with larger financial institutions while maintaining a personalized, community-oriented approach to lending and savings.

This is a legitimate but very small financial institution with $601,200 in assets and fewer than 95 members. Prospective members should note the specialized membership eligibility requirements tied to religious organization affiliation, limited branch infrastructure (one location), and minimal ATM access (zero ATMs reported). While they offer standard credit union services, the extremely small membership base and single branch location may limit service accessibility compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto loans (new and used vehicles at 0% APR)
Personal loans (11.50% APR on unsecured loans/lines of credit)
Mortgage loans (fixed-rate mortgages 15 years or less)
Online banking with bill pay functionality
Loan products with competitive rates and low fees
pros: 0% APR on new and used vehicle loans as of the reported rates
Not-for-profit structure allows lower fees compared to for-profit banks
Offers specialized financial solutions aligned with spiritual values for faith community members
Provides online banking, bill pay, and mobile apps (iOS/Android) for digital convenience
Competitive rates on unsecured credit cards (0% APR) and other credit products
Access to financial education and workshops for member financial literacy
Established institution with 67+ years of service history (since 1957)
Mortgage options including fixed-rate mortgages with online calculators
cons: Extremely limited membership of 95+ members—smallest possible customer base among credit unions
Single branch location in Shreveport only with zero ATMs, severely restricting accessibility
Restrictive membership eligibility limited to members of specific religious organizations only
No traditional checking accounts listed (only share draft accounts with 0+ members reported)
Minimal online presence with no independent website, only third-party profile listing
Very small asset base of $601,200 suggests limited loan capacity and service resources
best_for: Members of affiliated religious organizations seeking faith-aligned banking with community focus
Local Shreveport residents needing auto loans or personal loans from a faith-based credit union
Individuals seeking 0% APR vehicle financing (new or used) through a not-for-profit lender
Members wanting online banking, mobile apps, and basic savings accounts with low fees
diagnosis: Avenue Baptist Brotherhood Federal Credit Union is exclusively appropriate for members of affiliated religious organizations in the Shreveport area seeking a faith-aligned, not-for-profit alternative to commercial banks. The critical caveat is the combination of extremely limited membership eligibility, single-branch physical presence with no ATMs, and minimal scale that may constrain loan approval capacity and service availability compared to larger regional credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
